ddls:
CREATE TABLE "stores" (
  "store_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each store */,
  "store_name" TEXT /* Name of the store */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the store */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the store */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the store */,
  "social_media_links" TEXT /* Social media links for the store */,
  "logo_url" TEXT /* URL of the store's logo */,
  "store_description" TEXT /* Description of the store */,
  "opening_hours" TEXT /* Opening hours of the store */,
  "website_url" TEXT /* URL of the store's official website */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("store_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cards" (
  "card_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each card */,
  "card_name" TEXT /* Name of the card */,
  "card_type" TEXT /* Type of card (e.g., creature, spell) */,
  "card_rarity" TEXT /* Rarity of the card (e.g., common, rare) */,
  "set_name" TEXT /* Name of the set the card belongs to */,
  "artist" TEXT /* Name of the artist who designed the card */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the card was released */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the card */,
  "flavor_text" TEXT /* Flavor text or lore about the card */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("card_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventories" (
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inventory entry */,
  "store_id" INTEGER /* ID of the store that owns the inventory */,
  "card_id" INTEGER /* ID of the card in the inventory */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the card in stock */,
  "condition" TEXT /* Condition of the card (e.g., near mint, lightly played) */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the card */,
  "date_added" TEXT /* Date the card was added to the inventory */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the inventory was last updated */,
  "reorder_level" INTEGER /* Threshold for reordering the card */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier of the card */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventories_store_id FOREIGN KEY ("store_id") REFERENCES stores ("store_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventories_card_id FOREIGN KEY ("card_id") REFERENCES cards ("card_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who placed the order */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date the order was placed */,
  "total_cost" REAL /* Total cost of the order */,
  "shipping_method" TEXT /* Method of shipping */,
  "payment_status" TEXT /* Status of the payment (e.g., pending, completed, failed) */,
  "order_status" TEXT /* Status of the order (e.g., processing, shipped, delivered) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_items" (
  "order_item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order item */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order that the item belongs to */,
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* ID of the inventory entry for the item */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the item in the order */,
  "unit_price" REAL /* Price of each unit of the item */,
  "subtotal" REAL /* Subtotal for the item in the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_inventory_id FOREIGN KEY ("inventory_id") REFERENCES inventories ("inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the customer */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date the customer joined the platform */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date the customer last logged in */,
  "loyalty_points" INTEGER /* Loyalty points earned by the customer */,
  "newsletter_subscription" INTEGER /* Whether the customer is subscribed to the newsletter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who gave the rating */,
  "store_id" INTEGER /* ID of the store being rated */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the customer (e.g., 1-5 stars) */,
  "review" TEXT /* Review left by the customer */,
  "rating_date" TEXT /* Date the rating was given */,
  "helpful_votes" INTEGER /* Number of votes that found the review helpful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_store_id FOREIGN KEY ("store_id") REFERENCES stores ("store_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "payments" (
  "payment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each payment */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order that the payment belongs to */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method of payment (e.g., credit card, PayPal) */,
  "payment_date" TEXT /* Date the payment was made */,
  "transaction_id" TEXT /* Transaction ID from the payment provider */,
  "payment_status" TEXT /* Status of the payment (e.g., pending, completed, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("payment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_payments_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shipping" (
  "shipment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shipment */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order that the shipment belongs to */,
  "shipping_address" TEXT /* Address to which the shipment was sent */,
  "shipping_date" TEXT /* Date the shipment was sent */,
  "shipping_method" TEXT /* Method of shipping */,
  "tracking_number" TEXT /* Tracking number for the shipment */,
  "estimated_delivery_date" TEXT /* Estimated delivery date */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("shipment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shipping_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "returns" (
  "return_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each return */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order that the return belongs to */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the return */,
  "return_date" TEXT /* Date the return was made */,
  "return_status" TEXT /* Status of the return (e.g., pending, approved, refused) */,
  "refund_amount" REAL /* Amount refunded to the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("return_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_returns_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "store_id" INTEGER /* ID of the store that the analytics belong to */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the analytics */,
  "sales" INTEGER /* Number of sales made by the store */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated by the store */,
  "average_rating" REAL /* Average rating of the store */,
  "customer_satisfaction" TEXT /* Overall customer satisfaction score */,
  "most_popular_cards" TEXT /* Most popular cards sold by the store */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_store_id FOREIGN KEY ("store_id") REFERENCES stores ("store_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "promotions" (
  "promotion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each promotion */,
  "store_id" INTEGER /* ID of the store that offered the promotion */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the promotion */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the promotion */,
  "discount" REAL /* Discount offered by the promotion */,
  "promotion_name" TEXT /* Name of the promotion */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the promotion */,
  "promotion_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the promotion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("promotion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_promotions_store_id FOREIGN KEY ("store_id") REFERENCES stores ("store_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "newsletters" (
  "newsletter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each newsletter */,
  "store_id" INTEGER /* ID of the store that sent the newsletter */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the newsletter was sent */,
  "subject" TEXT /* Subject of the newsletter */,
  "body" TEXT /* Body of the newsletter */,
  "newsletter_name" TEXT /* Name of the newsletter */,
  "unsubscribe_link" TEXT /* Link for customers to unsubscribe from the newsletter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("newsletter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_newsletters_store_id FOREIGN KEY ("store_id") REFERENCES stores ("store_id")
)
